figures.caption: Table 1. Comparison of existing benchmarks. #Scenario indicates the number of categories, #Instr. reports the number of examples, Prompt specifies whether prompts are template-based (T), generated through an LLM (L) or taken from existing datasets (E), Layout indicates whether instructions include bounding boxes.

Table 3. Ranking for the six models under test on closed (C-Bench) and open set (O-Bench) benchmarks. *Stable Diffusion is reported as baseline since it does not have layout capabilities. Bold = best model, Underline = second best. sunified represents our unified score and ∆% is the performance delta between model’s score and top performer.

Figure 2. Performance by scenario and object count on C-Bench, measured by the unified score for the six models under test. SD = Stable Diffusion [34], SD CAG = Cross-Attention Guidance [6], G = GLIGEN [21], G BD = BoxDiff [39], G AR = Attention Refocusing [30], M = MIGC [41].

Figure 1. Overview of our evaluation framework. We construct the closed-set benchmark, automatically built by generating prompts and layouts, and the open-set benchmark derived from an existing dataset for visual grounding. A layout-guided diffusion model generates images, forming the evaluation set. The evaluation protocol assesses the images, providing text-alignment, layout-alignment and unified scores for both interpretability and consistent model ranking.
Figure 4. Performance breakdown by object count on O-Bench, measured with the unified score.

Figure 3. Performance breakdown by scenario (top) and number of objects in the prompt (bottom) on C-Bench, measured by text-alignment and layout-alignment score (resp. left, right). SD = Stable Diffusion [34], SD CAG = Cross-Attention Guidance [6], G = GLIGEN [21], G BD = BoxDiff [39], G AR = Attention Refocusing [30], M = MIGC [41].
Table 2. Templates used by the Prompt Generation Engine in different scenarios except complex compositions, shown with N = 4 objects.
